en:
At the age of eighty, an old man decided to learn Italian after retirement.
His family worried that he might be too old to remember new words.
He listened to recordings every day and read short phrases aloud.
Month by month, his pronunciation became clearer and more confident.
Soon he was brave enough to order food in simple Italian.
He proved that no one is ever too old to begin learning.
cn:
一位八十岁的老人退休后决定学习意大利语。
家人担心他年纪太大，记不住新单词。
他每天听录音，还大声朗读短句。
一个月又一个月，他的发音越来越清晰、自信。
不久他已经有足够勇气用简单意大利语点餐。
他证明了一个道理：学习从来不怕年纪大。

en:
A speedboat was racing along the Thames when it suddenly went out of control.
Tourists were taking photos as the boat began to swing dangerously.
The driver fought the wheel but could not steady the craft.
People on the shore shouted while rescue teams rushed over.
Police boats arrived within minutes and cleared nearby traffic.
Thanks to the quick response, a serious collision was avoided.
cn:
一艘快艇在泰晤士河上疾驰时突然失去控制。
快艇开始危险摇摆时，游客们还在拍照。
驾驶员拼命转动方向盘，却无法让船稳定下来。
岸边人群大声示警，救援队迅速赶来。
警方船只几分钟内到场，并疏散了附近船流。
由于反应及时，一次严重碰撞被成功避免。

en:
A man was accused of robbery, and the evidence first looked terrible for him.
He insisted that he had been in a theatre at the time of the crime.
Police checked his story with ticket staff and audience members.
By then, several witnesses had confirmed seeing him in his seat.
The timeline matched the show schedule exactly.
His perfect alibi cleared him, and investigators turned to another suspect.
cn:
一名男子被指控抢劫，起初证据对他非常不利。
他坚持说案发时自己正在剧院看戏。
警方随后向售票员和现场观众核实情况。
到那时，已有多位证人证实看见他坐在座位上。
时间线与演出安排完全吻合。
这个完美不在场证明洗清了他，调查转向了其他嫌疑人。

en:
Several boys exploring an old mine were trapped by falling rocks.
Before rescuers arrived, they had been stuck in darkness for hours.
Emergency teams entered carefully and tested the air first.
Heavy equipment was carried in and assembled in narrow passages.
After long effort, a blocked route was finally opened.
The boys were brought out safely, tired but alive.
cn:
几名男孩在旧矿洞探险时被落石困住。
救援到来前，他们已经在黑暗中被困了数小时。
应急队伍小心进入后，先检测洞内空气。
重型设备被运入狭窄通道并重新组装。
经过长时间努力，被堵路线终于被打通。
男孩们被安全救出，虽然疲惫但都活着。

en:
During a public speech, a minister made an embarrassing slip of the tongue.
He meant to support lower taxes but said the opposite phrase.
Reporters wrote the words down immediately and headlines spread fast.
Later his office clarified that it had only been a speaking error.
The host next day said that he had corrected himself.
The incident showed how one wrong word can reshape a whole message.
cn:
一次公开演讲中，一位部长出现了尴尬口误。
他本想表达支持减税，却说成了相反意思。
记者立刻记录原话，相关标题迅速传播。
随后他的办公室澄清，那只是一次说话失误。
第二天主持人转述说，他已经对该说法作出修正。
这件事说明，一个错词就可能改写整段信息。

en:
A giant fish arrived at a famous fish-and-chips shop by the coast.
News spread quickly, and customers came from nearby towns.
People asked whether one fish could feed a whole neighborhood.
The fish has been cut into many portions for different meals.
Some pieces were cooked at once, and others were frozen.
By evening the shop had turned an unusual catch into a local festival.
cn:
一条巨鱼被送到海边一家著名炸鱼薯条店。
消息很快传开，周边城镇的顾客纷纷赶来。
大家都在问，这一条鱼是否够整片社区吃。
这条鱼已经被分切成许多份用于不同餐食。
部分鱼块立刻下锅，另一部分被冷冻保存。
到傍晚，这次罕见渔获已经变成了一场本地节庆。

en:
Three men floated over London in the basket of a hot-air balloon.
At first the flight was smooth and full of excitement.
Then thick clouds hid the landmarks below and they lost direction.
They shouted down to people in the streets for help.
The answers were vague, and the balloon drifted between tall buildings.
Finally they landed safely in an open field and laughed at their adventure.
cn:
三个人乘热气球吊篮在伦敦上空漂浮。
起初飞行很顺利，大家都很兴奋。
后来厚云遮住了下方地标，他们一时迷失方向。
他们朝街上的行人大喊求助。
可得到的回答很模糊，气球继续在高楼间漂移。
最终他们安全降落在空地上，并笑谈这次冒险。

en:
A student policeman had been helping with traffic during his holiday duty.
He checked roads carefully and warned drivers about parking rules.
One busy afternoon, he parked his own car in a no-parking zone.
Another officer gave him a ticket before he noticed the sign.
He admitted his mistake and accepted the fine without arguing.
The story became a useful lesson about asking for trouble by breaking your own rules.
cn:
一名学生警察在假期执勤时一直协助交通管理。
他认真巡查道路，并提醒司机遵守停车规定。
某个忙碌下午，他却把自己的车停在了禁停区。
在他注意到标志前，另一名警员已经给他开了罚单。
他承认错误，没有争辩就接受了处罚。
这件事成了生动教材：违反自己执行的规则就是自找麻烦。

en:
The Statue of Liberty was presented as a noble gift from France to America.
It was built in sections and packed carefully before shipment.
The parts crossed the Atlantic and arrived in New York Harbor.
Engineers reassembled the statue piece by piece on its base.
Crowds celebrated when the monument was unveiled.
Since then, it has stood as a lasting symbol of friendship and freedom.
cn:
自由女神像是法国赠送给美国的一份高尚礼物。
它先分段建造，再被仔细装箱运输。
这些部件跨越大西洋，抵达纽约港。
工程师把雕像一块一块重新组装到基座上。
揭幕当天，现场人群热烈庆祝。
从那时起，它一直象征着友谊与自由。

en:
At a weekend gymnastics meet, young athletes warmed up from early morning.
Their coach said they had improved a lot this season.
Many of them have mastered moves that were once too difficult.
Before the event, they had been practicing balance and timing for months.
During competition, they performed with surprising confidence.
The judges believed they could become future champions with steady training.
cn:
周末体操赛上，小选手们从清晨就开始热身。
教练说他们这个赛季进步非常明显。
许多人已经掌握了过去太难完成的动作。
比赛前，他们已持续数月练习平衡和节奏。
正式上场时，他们展现出令人惊喜的自信。
评委认为，只要稳定训练，他们会成为未来冠军。

en:
After a long day in his clinic, a dentist fell asleep and had a bizarre dream.
In the fantasy, he was running with bulls through Pamplona streets.
At the same time, patients lined up and asked him to pull teeth.
He searched for his tools, but they kept disappearing at the worst moments.
He woke up exhausted and laughed at how strange it all felt.
He decided that if he rested earlier, he would have fewer crazy dreams.
cn:
忙完诊所的一整天后，一位牙医睡着并做了个离奇的梦。
在梦里，他在潘普洛纳街头和奔牛一起狂奔。
同时还有病人排队，催他给他们拔牙。
他四处找工具，却总在关键时刻消失不见。
醒来时他疲惫不堪，又觉得这一切十分荒诞。
他决定以后早点休息，这样就会少做些疯狂梦。

en:
On Halloween night, scary decorations were placed near a cemetery road.
In the dark, moving shadows made several passers-by panic.
Someone called the police and reported that the dead had returned.
Officers arrived and found only props blowing in the wind.
After the misunderstanding, the community gave a safety warning.
Organizers had better move frightening displays away from main streets next year.
cn:
万圣节夜里，墓地附近道路摆满了恐怖装饰。
黑暗中晃动的影子让几名路人惊慌失措。
有人报警，说“亡灵回来了”。
警员赶到后发现只是道具在风中摆动。
误会解除后，社区发布了安全提醒。
组织者明年最好把惊悚布景移离主干道。
